mainEntity.name: My site already ranks well in Google. Do I still need to change anything for voice search SEO?

acceptedAnswer.text:
If you already rank well in text search, you’re ahead, but you’re not automatically optimized for voice.

Voice assistants often pull different snippets and emphasize conversational questions, so you still need targeted adjustments.

**Key factors:**
- Review winning pages for question-based headings and short answers.
- Add FAQ sections that mirror how people speak their questions.
- Implement FAQPage and other relevant schemas for those answers.
- Check mobile speed and user experience, where voice queries originate.

Treat your existing rankings as leverage: start with your top URLs and adapt them for spoken queries instead of trying to optimize everything at once. This lets you capitalize on authority while refining format.

A thoughtful SEO partner will begin with your current strengths instead of ignoring them.
acceptedAnswer.description: Yes. Strong text rankings help, but voice assistants favor concise, conversational answers and structured data. You’ll want to update top-ranking pages with question-based headings, 40–60 word answers, FAQ sections, relevant schema, and solid mobile performance to fully capture voice search opportunities.
